gap_topic: Cross-JPY pairs (EUR/JPY, GBP/JPY, AUD/JPY, NZD/JPY) appear in the PM instrument-universe but are NOT in _JPY_CORRELATED frozenset at exposure_aggregator.py:46. If either paper loop ever generates a signal on a cross-JPY pair, the aggregator will treat that position's notional as NON-JPY-correlated — under-counting JPY tail risk. Not blocking for current USDJPY-only scope.
suggested_resolution: Confirm that for paper Phase 1 the only traded pair is USDJPY (no cross-JPY signals fire), OR amend the frozenset before enabling cross-JPY trading.

gap_topic: No verification was performed of WHETHER cross-host or symlink-based deployment topologies could defeat the fcntl advisory lock. fcntl.flock provides mutual exclusion only between processes on the same kernel sharing the same file. If deployment evolves to two-host topology (e.g., paper loops running in containers with separate filesystems), the lock becomes vacuous. Current topology (single workstation) is assumed and binding; any deployment change is a CRO re-review trigger.
suggested_resolution: Document single-host constraint in deployment runbook; add CRO re-review trigger for any container/multi-host deployment change.

gap_topic: NHT did not independently execute the gate suite (pytest/ruff/forbidden-phrases) to re-verify Wave-10 closures — relied on Wave-10 NHT re-verification artifact records. If the artifact text drifts from current code state, NHT Claim 3 verdict is conditional on the artifact's accuracy.
suggested_resolution: At next gate, NHT or QD should re-execute the full test suite to verify current code state matches Wave-10 artifact records.

gap_topic: Whether the firm has explicitly adopted the PM-crystallized CF-T9 disclosure wording as binding policy (as opposed to a sample wording satisfying the NHT requirement) is not stated in any artifact NHT read. This affects the interpretation of SD-6 done_when criterion strictness.
suggested_resolution: CEO or PM should explicitly record at ratification time whether the PM-crystallized clause is adopted as binding firm policy wording, or whether substantive conformance is the operative test.

gap_topic: The redacted CRO artifact shows seven BC-8-LIFT-COND-* labels, but only BC-8-LIFT-COND-7 survives label redaction. PR cannot independently verify which three of the seven PM enumerated inline in SD-2 done_when. Resolving F-002 requires PM to either name all seven by ID or explicitly incorporate them by reference.
suggested_resolution: CONSENSUS SD-2 body must either enumerate all seven BC-8-LIFT-COND-* IDs explicitly or contain a by-reference clause adopting all seven from the CRO artifact.

gap_topic: Wave-10 binding wording on BC-8 (CEO ratification 2026-05-06) does not enumerate the seven CRO BC-8-LIFT-COND constraints. Whether those constraints were considered ratified on 2026-05-06 along with the lift, or are a separate Wave-2 emission specific to 2026-05-10 paper-launch, is a lineage question PM must resolve in SD-2 body.
suggested_resolution: PM should clarify in CONSENSUS whether the seven CRO constraints (emitted 2026-05-10) are additive to the 2026-05-06 ratification or are the complete operative set for paper-launch authorization.

gap_topic: Whether LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB semantics survive tmux SIGTERM gracefully (specifically: does try/finally block run when tmux session is killed)
suggested_resolution: CRO must review Step 2.4 and confirm lock release behavior post-launch

gap_topic: Exact log line format emitted by account_key_parity gate on success
suggested_resolution: Runbook checks for absence of VIOLATION; if gate passes silently (no log), verification is indirect

gap_topic: Whether drawdown ladder snapshot is logged at startup or only on trigger
suggested_resolution: Runbook checks for drawdown keyword in log; if not present, status is unknown but not a blocker

gap_topic: Exact format of first cycle log entry (varies by strategy; may be different for vt vs carry_fred)
suggested_resolution: Runbook uses conservative check: log must grow by >=1 line in 10 seconds; exact format not required

gap_topic: Behavior of Python's try/finally under tmux kill-session in macOS (does tmux default send SIGTERM or SIGHUP first; does Python convert this to a KeyboardInterrupt that runs finally)
suggested_resolution: Operator should prefer 'tmux send-keys C-c' for graceful shutdown over 'tmux kill-session'. Audit-trail-bearing shutdown documented in amendment 8.

suggested_resolution: Add to Step 1.5 (or 1.6): 'ls -la data/paper_account_key_lock.json; if exists, check its age and contents. If it predates the current Saxo token issuance, reset via --reset-account-key-lock NEW_KEY --confirm-account-reset before launch.'

gap_topic: Whether KillSwitch.__post_init__ stale-HALTED check would block startup in current state (audit log files from prior dispatch testing)
suggested_resolution: Pre-flight check in Amendment 2 covers this.

gap_topic: Whether Strategy #4 (ML) requires model serving infrastructure (e.g., feature pipelines, versioned model artifacts, online inference) that has no current interface in core/interfaces.py

Whether walkforward.py supports multi-strategy portfolio-level evaluation. File was not read in detail. Routing to Quant Developer and Head of Quant Research for portfolio walk-forward design.

gap_topic: What specific algorithm class strategy #3 (intraday-classical) is. If it trades USDJPY or GBPUSD at 4H, the Position.strategy_id gap (REM-1) becomes critical before any paper deployment.

Cross-host flock caveat: fcntl.flock does not work across NFS. If strategies are ever deployed across hosts, the dispatch-lock mechanism fails silently. Not a current risk (single-host) but a scaling boundary.
Quantified lock contention probability at 4H frequency with 4 strategies. Asserted as operationally acceptable at daily frequency; 4H has 6 bar closes per day.
Saxo session boundary — is one session = one bearer token, one IP, or one process? Affects whether N=4 paper loops sharing one bearer share one 120/min bucket.
suggested_resolution: Explicit confirmation from Saxo developer support before N=3 deployment

gap_topic: Empirical SKIP_DISPATCH_LOCK_BUSY rate at N=2 (existing) — never measured. Required as a baseline before N=3.
suggested_resolution: Add SKIP_DISPATCH_LOCK_BUSY counter instrumentation; measure over 1-week paper run

gap_topic: Duplication metric reproducibility and methodology dispute: QD implementation report described reduction as minimal (attributed to shim symmetry, delta -2 lines); PR's independent line count found delta -2 lines (466 to 464); orchestrator measurement using CTO COND-A6 methodology found 72.9% to 72.7% (0.2 pp net). Methodology dispute resolved by orchestrator measurement, which matches PR. Resolution: REM-2-followup-2 dispatch with shim removal and strengthened methodology documentation.
suggested_resolution: REM-2-followup-2 dispatch: remove backward-compat shims from both scripts; update tests to call BaseRunner interface directly; re-run orchestrator measurement; target < 557 unique lines (pre-extraction baseline).

gap_topic: Empirical decay rate of intraday FX session-open / opening-range-breakout momentum in liquid majors 2010–2025 — is the edge still present post-2020 and at what magnitude? Load-bearing for H1 G6 non-stationarity kill-risk and honest IR.
suggested_resolution: Published intraday-FX session-momentum anomaly decay/half-life literature; EURUSD+GBPUSD London-open momentum persistence post-2020.

gap_topic: Whether cross-pair signal correlation (rho_bar) for a pooled session-momentum signal is closer to 0.35 (G5 assumption) or higher — if USD-leg sharing pushes rho_bar toward 0.5+, H1 effective-N drops and slips toward STRETCH.
suggested_resolution: Typical intraday cross-pair return/signal correlation among USD majors at the 1h horizon during London/NY opens.

gap_topic: Whether a single CB decision genuinely yields ~2 semi-independent intraday sub-events (open-reaction vs press-conference-reaction) — this decomposition is the ONLY thing that gets H2 under 2yr and may be a data-mining artifact.
suggested_resolution: Intraday price-discovery profile around scheduled CB rate decisions — single-shock vs multi-stage repricing at the 1h horizon.

gap_topic: Dukascopy 1h bar density per pair (actual bars/year at 1h resolution) not yet known — min_bars thresholds in CTO-D2 may need revision on first data-land.

gap_topic: Per-pair typical spread_median_pips from Dukascopy 1h data not yet confirmed — CTO-D2 spread ceilings are estimates; could be too tight or too loose.

gap_topic: The ingest counts and LOGS n_crossed_clipped per bar but does NOT PERSIST a clipped fraction or tick_count to the parquet — R-1 (low-tick fabricated spread) and R-2 (crossed-tick under-charge) cannot be gated per-bar from the parquet alone.
suggested_resolution: QD add a per-bar tick_count and clipped_fraction column to _resample_ticks_to_1h so the coverage gate can EXCLUDE low-tick and high-clip bars.

gap_topic: Per-pair plausible spread bands stated as 0.1-8 pip placeholders — real per-pair P50/P90 spread distributions not yet known (backfill in flight).

gap_topic: Empirical 1h residual-return autocorrelation decay length is NOT yet measured; embargo=480 is a conservative default and the 24-bar floor a backstop. The ACF measurement MUST run before any DSR/power claim.
suggested_resolution: QD runs ACF measurement (D2) as a required pre-backtest step; all effective-N and embargo numbers are provisional until measured.

gap_topic: Spread-column correctness in commit 96f6f84 not verified end-to-end (python forbidden to NHT; cannot run ingest). Correctness is a RESEARCH_REQUEST — required before any net-of-TCA claim on 1h data is trustworthy.
